tool_input.old_string:
# Validate <vault-name>; on invalid input, echoes Error: ... to stderr and returns non-zero.
vk_validate_vault_name() {
  local name="$1"
  if [[ "$name" =~ / ]]; then
    vk_error "provide the vault name only (e.g. 'SystemDesign'), not owner/repo."
    return 1
  fi
  if ! [[ "$name" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then
    vk_error "vault name must contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores."
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ${#name} -gt 64 ]; then
    vk_error "vault name must be 64 characters or less."
    return 1
  fi
  return 0
}
tool_input.new_string:
# Validate <vault-name>; on invalid input, echoes Error: ... to stderr and returns non-zero.
vk_validate_vault_name() {
  local name="$1"
  if [[ "$name" =~ / ]]; then
    vk_error "provide the vault name only (e.g. 'SystemDesign'), not owner/repo."
    return 1
  fi
  if ! [[ "$name" =~ ^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$ ]]; then
    vk_error "vault name must contain only letters, numbers, hyphens, and underscores."
    return 1
  fi
  if [ ${#name} -gt 64 ]; then
    vk_error "vault name must be 64 characters or less."
    return 1
  fi
  return 0
}

# Add npm's global bin directory to PATH so freshly installed tools (e.g. claude)
# are visible without opening a new terminal.
vk_refresh_npm_path() {
  command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1 || return 0
  local prefix
  prefix=$(npm config get prefix 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  [ -z "$prefix" ] || [ "$prefix" = "undefined" ] && return 0
  local bin_dir
  if command -v cygpath >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Windows Git Bash: npm prefix is a Windows path; executables live in the prefix dir itself.
    bin_dir=$(cygpath -u "$prefix" 2>/dev/null) || return 0
  else
    bin_dir="$prefix/bin"
  fi
  [ -d "$bin_dir" ] && export PATH="$bin_dir:$PATH"
  return 0
}

# Ensure Claude Code CLI is available, offering to install it if not.
# Refreshes PATH after install so the caller can use `claude` immediately.
# Returns 0 if claude is available, 1 if not (user declined or install failed).
vk_ensure_claude() {
  command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
  local _INSTALL
  echo "" >&2
  read -r -p "  Claude Code CLI not found. Install it now? [y/N] " _INSTALL
  if ! [[ "${_INSTALL:-}" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
    return 1
  fi
  echo "  Installing Claude Code CLI..." >&2
  if npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code; then
    vk_refresh_npm_path
    if command -v claude >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      return 0
    fi
    vk_warning "Installed, but 'claude' is not on PATH yet — open a new terminal and re-run."
  else
    vk_warning "Install failed — run manually: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code"
  fi
  return 1
}

# Ensure git is available, offering to install it if not.
# Returns 0 if git is available, 1 if not (user declined or install failed).
vk_ensure_git() {
  command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
  vk_error "git is required but was not found."
  echo "" >&2
  local _INSTALL
  if command -v cygpath >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # Windows Git Bash
    if cmd //c "winget --version" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      read -r -p "  Install Git for Windows via winget? [y/N] " _INSTALL
      if [[ "${_INSTALL:-}" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
        cmd //c "winget install --id Git.Git -e --accept-package-agreements --accept-source-agreements"
        local WIN_DIR POSIX_DIR
        for WIN_DIR in \
          "${PROGRAMFILES:-C:/Program Files}/Git/bin" \
          "${PROGRAMFILES:-C:/Program Files}/Git/cmd"; do
          POSIX_DIR=$(cygpath -u "$WIN_DIR" 2>/dev/null || true)
          [ -n "$POSIX_DIR" ] && [ -d "$POSIX_DIR" ] && export PATH="$POSIX_DIR:$PATH"
        done
        command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
        vk_warning "Git installed — open a new terminal and re-run."
        return 1
      fi
    fi
    echo "  Install from: https://git-scm.com/download/win" >&2
  elif command -v brew >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    read -r -p "  Install git via brew? [y/N] " _INSTALL
    if [[ "${_INSTALL:-}" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
      brew install git
      command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
    fi
  elif command -v xcode-select >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "  Install git via: xcode-select --install" >&2
  elif command -v apt-get >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    read -r -p "  Install git via apt? [y/N] " _INSTALL
    if [[ "${_INSTALL:-}" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
      sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y git
      command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
    fi
  elif command -v dnf >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    read -r -p "  Install git via dnf? [y/N] " _INSTALL
    if [[ "${_INSTALL:-}" =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
      sudo dnf install -y git
      command -v git >/dev/null 2>&1 && return 0
    fi
  else
    echo "  Install from: https://git-scm.com" >&2
  fi
  return 1
}

# Try to push HEAD to the current branch's upstream. If rejected (branch protection
# or insufficient rights), create a feature branch, push it, and open a PR.
#
# Usage: vk_push_or_pr <branch-prefix> <pr-title> <pr-body>
#
# Sets VK_LAST_PUSH_MODE to "direct" or "pr" so the caller can print the
# appropriate completion message. Returns 1 (and exits via set -e) on failure.
VK_LAST_PUSH_MODE=""

vk_push_or_pr() {
  VK_LAST_PUSH_MODE=""
  local prefix="$1" pr_title="$2" pr_body="$3"

  if git push 2>&1; then
    VK_LAST_PUSH_MODE="direct"
    return 0
  fi

  vk_warning "Push to main was rejected (branch may be protected or you lack write access)."
  echo ""
  local BRANCH="${prefix}-$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S)"
  echo "Creating branch '$BRANCH' for a pull request..."

  git branch "$BRANCH"
  git reset --hard '@{u}'
  git checkout "$BRANCH"

  if ! git push -u origin "$BRANCH" 2>&1; then
    vk_error "Could not push branch '$BRANCH'."
    echo "  Push manually: git push -u origin $BRANCH" >&2
    return 1
  fi

  if command -v gh >/dev/null 2>&1 && gh auth status >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    gh pr create \
      --title "$pr_title" \
      --body "$pr_body" \
      --base main \
      --head "$BRANCH" \
      || vk_warning "PR creation failed — open one manually."
  else
    local repo_slug
    repo_slug=$(git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null \
      | sed -E 's|.*github\.com[:/]([^/]+/[^/.]+)(\.git)?/?$|\1|')
    echo "Open a pull request manually:"
    echo "  https://github.com/$repo_slug/compare/main...$BRANCH"
  fi

  VK_LAST_PUSH_MODE="pr"
  return 0
}
